message: test(perf): gate perf baselines in CI (br-frankenpandas-ing6)

files.patch:
@@ -354,7 +354,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ijpu","title":"fp-conformance: phase2c_artifact_test_lock poison-tolerance (18 test cascade)","description":"Observed: cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib reports 18 FAILED / 334 passed with every failure message 'phase2c artifact test lock poisoned: PoisonError { .. }'.

Root: crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:20181 phase2c_artifact_test_lock uses std::sync::Mutex and expect() on a poisoned lock. Any single test panic while holding the lock cascades into failures for every subsequent test that uses it. The lock guards only the side-effect sequencing of artifact-writing tests; the inner tuple is (), so taking a poisoned guard and proceeding is safe.

Fix: replace .expect() with .unwrap_or_else(|err| err.into_inner()) to recover the guard through the poison. Downstream call sites don't need changes. 
Tests: cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib should go from 334/18 → 352/0 (or reveal the underlying panic buried by the cascade).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:29:48.729395236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:35:16.486344833Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:35:16.483797039Z","close_reason":"shipped 2c98ff2: phase2c_artifact_test_lock now recovers poisoned guards via into_inner() (safe because inner is ()). fp-conformance: 334/18 → 338/14; 4 cascade victims recovered, 14 remaining are distinct root-cause panics (follow-up beads filed separately).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ikc","title":"Add Series.at_time conformance test coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T07:50:42.475950267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T07:51:59.477848552Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T07:51:59.475573345Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ikkn","title":"fp-conformance: live pandas oracle for DataFrame.apply sem|nunique|prod aliases","description":"Add real pandas-oracle coverage for DataFrame.apply('sem'|'nunique', axis=0) and DataFrame.apply('prod'|'product', axis=1). Cover singleton-NaN SEM behavior and row-wise null-skipping product parity through fp-conformance live oracle cases and phase2c artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-22T05:30:08.901740844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:55:33.994590456Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:55:33.992720106Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","testing"]}
-{"id":"frankenpandas-ing6","title":"[HIGH] ci: no performance regression gate — 'cargo bench' is a no-op, perf_baselines.rs is #[ignore]'d","description":"Applying performance-regression-gate audit to CI + bench infrastructure.

**Observation.**
- ci.yml line 46 runs `cargo bench`. Workspace has NO `[[bench]]` targets in any Cargo.toml (grep for `\[\[bench\]\]` or `criterion` returns empty). So `cargo bench` is currently a no-op smoke test that always passes.
- crates/fp-conformance/tests/perf_baselines.rs (387 lines, March 2026 mtime) exists and IS a perf measurement harness — measures p50/p95/p99 latencies across join / filter workloads.
- BUT: perf_baselines.rs tests are marked `#[ignore]` by default (see the rustdoc header comment). CI does not pass `--include-ignored`. So perf_baselines never runs in CI either.
- Even when run, perf_baselines only `eprintln!` the results. There are zero `assert!(p95 < Duration::from_millis(X))` calls. It's a human-readable benchmark, not a regression gate.
- No stored baseline file (no `artifacts/perf/baseline.json` or similar). Diffing against prior runs is impossible.

**Consequence.** A 10x slowdown in any hot path lands silently. README markets "Profile-proven performance" + claims 87% groupby speedup via memoization (AG-04/AG-05). Nothing enforces that the speedup persists. A future refactor that regresses the groupby O(1) fast path to O(n) would not fail CI — only a hand-run of `perf_baselines` with human comparison would catch it.

**Severity.** HIGH — core product claim is unguarded against regression. Performance claims that aren't measured in CI become theater.

**Fix shape:**
1. Convert perf_baselines.rs to assertion form: each benchmark gains `assert!(p95 < SOME_BUDGET)`. Budgets live in an `assets/perf_budgets.json` committed to the repo. Exceeding budget = fail CI.
2. Remove `#[ignore]` from perf_baselines.rs so tests run in `cargo test --workspace --all-targets`. Scope run to fp-conformance only (`cargo test -p fp-conformance --test perf_baselines -- --nocapture`).
3. OR (preferred): add a real `[[bench]]` block using `criterion` crate. Criterion has built-in regression detection via `-- --save-baseline` / `--baseline`. CI:
     step: cargo bench --bench perf -- --save-baseline pr
     step: cargo bench --bench perf -- --baseline main --threshold 5%
     step: fail on > 5% regression
4. Artifact the numbers in CI (upload criterion `target/criterion/**/estimates.json`) so trending is visible over time.
5. Remove the stub `cargo bench` line from ci.yml (currently misleading — it appears to run a benchmark gate but doesn't).

Blast radius: perf_baselines.rs (+30 assertions), assets/perf_budgets.json (+1 file), ci.yml (replace line 46). If going with criterion: crates/fp-conformance/benches/perf.rs (+~200 lines), Cargo.toml (+criterion dep).

Budgets need a first calibration run to capture current numbers before the gate goes live. Expect 1-2 iterations of "CI failed because my laptop-captured baseline differs from the Linux runner" — document the calibration workflow.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:27:46.851711998Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:27:46.851711998Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","performance","regression-gate","review"]}

     step: cargo bench --bench perf -- --save-baseline pr

     step: fail on > 5% regression
4. Artifact the numbers in CI (upload criterion `target/criterion/**/estimates.json`) so trending is visible over time.

Expect 1-2 iterations of "CI failed because my laptop-captured baseline differs from the Linux runner" — document the calibration workflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:27:46.851711998Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:21:33.159426153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:21:33.157057426Z","close_reason":"Replaced no-op cargo bench with a real budgeted perf regression gate and committed perf budgets","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","performance","regression-gate","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ip24","title":"fp-frame DataFrame.keys alias for columns parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:35:15.693920619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:35:56.001293633Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T09:35:55.999429703Z","close_reason":"Already implemented at fp-frame/src/lib.rs:14720 (DataFrame::keys returns MultiIndexOrIndex)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ipz","title":"Add DataFrame kurtosis/sum packet conformance coverage","description":"Complete DataFrame.kurtosis and adjacent DataFrame.sum fp-conformance harness/oracle wiring, add or correct packet fixtures, and verify via RCH.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:32:07.576263109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:56:12.265017074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:56:12.263196990Z","close_reason":"DataFrame sum coverage added in commit 818e735","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-iq9l","title":"Add Series str startswith/endswith/replace conformance coverage","description":"Expand Series.str accessor conformance with fixtures and harness support for startswith, endswith, and replace operations. Target: 6+ new fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:12:54.880084213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:17:11.985638390Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:17:11.983818389Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -55,8 +55,8 @@ jobs:
       - name: Conformance tests
         run: cargo test -p fp-conformance -- --nocapture
 
-      - name: Bench harness smoke
-        run: cargo bench
+      - name: Performance regression gate
+        run: cargo test -p fp-conformance --test perf_baselines -- --nocapture --ignored --skip perf_run_all_baselines
 
       - name: Phase2C gate check
         run: ./scripts/phase2c_gate_check.sh

@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+{
+  "df_add_df_aligned_100k (5 cols, same index)": { "p95_secs": 1.2 },
+  "df_add_scalar_100k (5 cols)": { "p95_secs": 0.3 },
+  "df_eq_scalar_100k (5 cols)": { "p95_secs": 0.32 },
+  "filter_boolean_mask_100k (5 cols, 50% selectivity)": { "p95_secs": 0.75 },
+  "filter_boolean_mask_10k (5 cols, 50% selectivity)": { "p95_secs": 0.15 },
+  "head(1000)_100k (5 cols)": { "p95_secs": 0.02 },
+  "join_inner_100k (50% overlap)": { "p95_secs": 1.7 },
+  "join_inner_10k (50% overlap)": { "p95_secs": 0.18 },
+  "join_left_10k (50% overlap)": { "p95_secs": 0.22 },
+  "join_outer_100k (50% overlap)": { "p95_secs": 3.2 },
+  "join_outer_10k (50% overlap)": { "p95_secs": 0.26 },
+  "series_add_aligned_10k (50% overlap)": { "p95_secs": 0.25 },
+  "tail(1000)_100k (5 cols)": { "p95_secs": 0.02 }
+}
